data.title: WillScot retains adviser to assist with activist investor matters
data.description:
WillScot Holdings (NASDAQ:WSC) disclosed that it has retained an adviser to assist on activist investor matters.

The maker of mobile offices disclosed the adviser as a new risk factor in its 10-K on Thursday.

"For example, we have retained, and may in the future need to retain, the services of various professionals to advise us on activist investor matters, including legal, financial and communications advisors, the costs of which may negatively impact our future financial results," WillScot disclosed in the filing.

The disclosure confirms a report from Semafor last month that the company hired bankers to help it deal with an activist investor. The publication reported that WillScot (NASDAQ:WSC) has fielded takeover interest from several private-equity shops. At least one of the PE firms has engaged bankers to assist in a possible offer.

WillScot (NASDAQ:WSC) agreed to mutually terminate a planned $3.8 billion takeover of McGrath RentCorp (MGRC) in September after the parties said there was no commercially reasonable path to clear the necessary regulatory requirements for the deal. Last January, WillScot (WSC) agreed to acquire B2B rental company McGrath.

data.title: First meeting of CDC vaccine advisory panel under RFK Jr. postponed
data.description:
A meeting of the CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP), which provides guidance on recommended vaccines and for which populations, scheduled for later this month has been postponed.

It was scheduled for Feb. 26-28, and was set to discuss vaccines including those for flu, RSV, COVID-19, meningococcal, and HPV, according to an agenda. CDC scientists were set to make presentations, as were representatives of Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY), GSK (NYSE:GSK), and Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA).

A message on top of the ACIP homepage states the postponement is "to accommodate public comment in advance of the meeting." An HHS spokesman did not provide STAT News, which first broke the news, with any clear indication of when the meeting would be rescheduled.

STAT reported that HHS never formally authorized the meeting to proceed. All advisory committee meetings where voting takes place require a period of time for the public to submit comments, and a gateway was never opened for submissions.

In the past, HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has opposed COVID vaccine recommendations and claimed childhood immunizations can cause autism.

On Feb. 18, in his first comments to HHS staffers since winning confirmation, RFK Jr. said that he would scrutinize topics that "were formerly taboo or insufficiently scrutinized," such as childhood vaccine schedules.

Other vaccine makers: Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), Merck (NYSE:MRK), and Novavax (NASDAQ:NVAX).

data.title: Marathon Bancorp announces commencement of stock offering
data.description:
• Marathon Bancorp (OTCPK:MBBC) on Thursday said it is commencing its stock offering in connection with the proposed conversion of Marathon MHC from the mutual holding company structure to the fully stock holding company form of organization.
  • The company is offering for sale up to 1.89M shares of common stock, subject to increase to up to 2.18M shares of common stock at a purchase price of $10.00 per share.
  • The company's common stock is expected to be listed on the Nasdaq under the symbol "MBBC" upon the completion of the conversion.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Citi drops diversity goals, 'DEI' term after Trump order - report
data.description:
After President Donald Trump ordered the federal government and government contractors to scrub diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives, Citigroup (NYSE:C) is ending such workplace representation goals and its requirement to interview job candidates from diverse backgrounds, according to a media report on Thursday.

The bank will no longer espouse "aspirational representation goals," except as required by local law, and will drop its practice of ensuring diversity in job candidates and interview panels, Bloomberg News reported, citing a memo from CEO Jane Fraser.

Its "Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion and Talent Management" team will be renamed the "Talent Management and Engagement" team, she said.

The recent changes in US federal government policy, including new requirements that apply to all federal contractors, call for changes to some of the global strategies and programs we’ve used to attract and support colleagues from various backgrounds,” she wrote in the memo.

Citi facilitates much of the U.S. government's payment network domestically and internationally, Bloomberg said, making it a major federal contractor.

A raft of other major corporations have also scrapped their diversity programs due to political and legal pressures. Other companies that have abandoned diversity goals, citing Trump's executive order, include Booz Allen Hamilton, Deloitte, and Accenture.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Everyday People announces debt reduction of $14.9M since July 18, 2024
data.description:
• Everyday People Financial (TSXV:EPF:CA) (OTCQB:EPFCF) on Thursday announced a reduction of about $14.9 million in debt with a combined annualized savings in interest expense of $1.7 million from July 18, 2024, to February 21, 2025.
  • Transitioning its EP Homes business line to the company's new Borrowed Down Payment Program, has resulted in a reduction in debt as a direct result of selling EP Homes inventory to clients eliminating high-cost debt off the balance sheet of $8.3 million, or 73% in its outstanding EP Homes debt, decreasing from $11.4 million as of July 18, 2024, to $3.1 million as of February 21, 2025.
  • Annualized interest expense savings are estimated to be $1.0 million.
  • In addition, the prior settlement agreements entered into by the company on September 30, 2024, January 17, and 28, 2025, resulted in a $6.6 million debt reduction with annualized interest expense savings of $720,000.
  • The company announced that it is preparing an application to graduate from the TSX Venture Exchange to the Toronto Stock Exchange.
  • The company also said it has approved the grant of an aggregate of 570,000 restricted share units to certain officers and management pursuant to its omnibus share incentive plan, for their service during the 2024 fiscal year.
  • Source: Press Release
